title: TAR File Extractor
description: Extract files from TAR archives online instantly and securely.

faq.question: How do I extract a TAR file?
faq.answer: Upload your TAR file to Converigo, and the contents will be extracted and ready for download immediately.

faq.question: Is the extraction secure?
faq.answer: Yes, files are processed securely on our servers and automatically deleted after extraction.

faq.question: What file sizes are supported?
faq.answer: TAR files up to 500MB can be extracted instantly on Converigo.

faq.question: What is the difference between TAR and ZIP?
faq.answer: TAR is a traditional Unix archiver that preserves file permissions and structure. ZIP includes compression by default.

faq.question: Can I extract TAR.GZ files?
faq.answer: Yes, both plain TAR and compressed TAR.GZ files are fully supported.

faq.question: Are permissions preserved?
faq.answer: Yes, file permissions and ownership information are preserved during extraction when available.

faq.question: What if extraction fails?
faq.answer: Ensure the TAR file is not corrupted and try re-uploading if extraction fails.

faq.question: Can I extract TAR files on mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, Converigo works on all modern browsers and devices.

about_formats.title: What is a TAR file?
about_formats.text: TAR (Tape Archive) is a traditional Unix archiver that bundles multiple files into a single archive without compression. It's widely used in Linux and Unix systems.

about_formats.title: Why extract a TAR file?
about_formats.text: Extracting TAR files allows you to access individual files, preserve Unix permissions, and view the original folder structure.

about_formats.title: TAR vs compression
about_formats.text: TAR is typically used with compression (like gzip) for distribution. Plain TAR files are uncompressed archives.

cta.title: Extract TAR files in seconds
cta.text: Upload your TAR archive and get all files instantly.
